title: Hierarchical Deep Research with Local-Web RAG: Toward Automated System-Level Materials Discovery
abstract: We present a long-horizon, hierarchical deep research (DR) agent designed for complex materials and device discovery problems that exceed the scope of existing Machine Learning (ML) surrogates and closed-source commercial agents. Our framework instantiates a locally deployable DR instance that integrates local retrieval-augmented generation with large language model reasoners, enhanced by a Deep Tree of Research (DToR) mechanism that adaptively expands and prunes research branches to maximize coverage, depth, and coherence. We systematically evaluate across 27 nanomaterials/device topics using a large language model (LLM)-as-judge rubric with five web-enabled state-of-the-art models as jurors. In addition, we conduct dry-lab validations on five representative tasks, where human experts use domain simulations (e.g., density functional theory, DFT) to verify whether DR-agent proposals are actionable. Results show that our DR agent produces reports with quality comparable to--and often exceeding--those of commercial systems (ChatGPT-5-thinking/o3/o4-mini-high Deep Research) at a substantially lower cost, while enabling on-prem integration with local data and tools.
ai_for_science_reason: The paper presents an AI-driven framework for materials discovery using hierarchical deep research agents, integrating LLMs and local retrieval-augmented generation.
implementation_justification: The framework is locally deployable and integrates with existing tools and data.
review_justification: The paper includes systematic evaluations and comparisons with state-of-the-art models.
benchmark_justification: The DR agent is compared against commercial systems and evaluated on multiple topics.
hypothesis_generation_justification: The DR agent adaptively expands research branches to generate hypotheses.
scientific_discovery_justification: The agent is designed for system-level materials discovery, a core scientific discovery process.
experiment_design_justification: The framework includes dry-lab validations to verify the actionability of proposals.
material_discovery_justification: The primary focus of the paper is on materials and device discovery.
molecular_design_justification: While not the main focus, molecular design is a component of the materials discovery process.
drug_discovery_justification: The paper does not specifically address drug discovery.
causal_discovery_justification: Causal discovery is not explicitly mentioned but may be inferred from hypothesis generation.
biomedical_discovery_justification: The focus is on materials science rather than biomedical applications.
idea_development_justification: The adaptive expansion and pruning of research branches support idea development.
paper_title: Hierarchical Deep Research with Local-Web RAG: Toward Automated System-Level Materials Discovery

objective.answer: The primary objective of the paper is to introduce a hierarchical deep-research (DR) agent designed for complex materials and device discovery problems, aiming to exceed the capabilities of existing machine learning surrogates and commercial agents.
objective.evidence: To address this gap, we introduce a hierarchical deep-research (DR) agent purpose-built for long-horizon scientific inquiry.

knowledge_gap.answer: Existing approaches face limitations in unstructured knowledge regimes, particularly in system-level optimization and design for complex scientific workflows.
knowledge_gap.evidence: Existing approaches typically face limitations in this unstructured knowledge regime.

answer: A locally deployable framework for scientific deep research that surpasses commercial state-of-the-art solutions.
A hierarchical orchestration (DToR) that improves synthesis quality over single-instance DR.
An evaluation program combining anchored rubric scores, repeated A/B duels, and dry-lab validation.
evidence: A democratized, locally deployable framework for scientific deep research, enabling materials researchers to exercise fine-grained control over cost and preferences using open-source LLMs while achieving performance that surpasses commercial state-of-the-art solutions in most cases.

A hierarchical orchestration (DToR) that yields robust gains over single-instance DR across LLM backbones and local data budgets, supported by comprehensive factorial and targeted component ablations.
An evaluation program combining anchored rubric scores, repeated A/B duels, and dry-lab validation to assess synthesis quality.

inspirational_papers.answer:
- Montoya et al. (2020) Autonomous intelligent agents for accelerated materials discovery. (Papers with limitations addressed by this work)
- Merchant et al. (2023) Scaling deep learning for materials discovery. (Papers with limitations addressed by this work)
inspirational_papers.evidence: Existing approaches typically face limitations in this unstructured knowledge regime [8, 9, 10, 11] (Detailed in Appendix A).

steps.step: Introduce a hierarchical deep-research (DR) agent for long-horizon scientific inquiry.
steps.evidence: Given a natural-language inquiry, the system instantiates a locally deployable DR instance.

steps.step: Expand the DR instance into a Deep Tree of Research (DToR).
steps.evidence: We further expand the DR instance into a Deep Tree of Research (DToR) where each instance is treated as a node in a tree-structured workflow.

steps.step: Evaluate synthesis quality using a programmatic evaluation harness.
steps.evidence: Our goal is to measure synthesis quality by a programmatic evaluation harness (anchored rubric, A/B preferences) plus dry-lab checks.

tools.name: Deep Tree of Research (DToR)
tools.description: Used for hierarchical orchestration and improving synthesis quality.
tools.evidence: A hierarchical orchestration (DToR) that yields robust gains over single-instance DR across LLM backbones and local data budgets.

evaluation_metrics.name: Anchored rubric scores
evaluation_metrics.application: Used to evaluate synthesis quality across model backbones and local-corpus budgets.
evaluation_metrics.evidence: An evaluation program combining anchored rubric scores, repeated A/B duels, and dry-lab validation to assess synthesis quality.

methods.name: Hypothesis or Idea Generation
methods.description: The system produces candidate hypotheses or new research ideas from prior knowledge or external input.
methods.evidence: We prompt the LLM to generate testable hypotheses using domain-specific concepts derived from structured data.

methods.name: Experimental design generation
methods.description: The approach includes producing experimental protocols, configurations, or evaluation strategies.
methods.evidence: Our model proposes complete experimental setups including dataset split, evaluation metrics, and variables.

areas.name: Chemical Sciences
areas.description: The paper focuses on materials and device discovery in the chemical sciences domain.
areas.evidence: Our framework instantiates a locally deployable DR instance that integrates local retrieval-augmented generation with large language model reasoners, enhanced by a Deep Tree of Research (DToR) mechanism.

areas.name: Applied Sciences & Engineering
areas.description: The proposed system is aimed at improving materials discovery workflows.
areas.evidence: A democratized, locally deployable framework for scientific deep research, enabling materials researchers to exercise fine-grained control over cost and preferences.

performance_summary.summary: The proposed DR agent produces reports with quality comparable to—and often exceeding—those of commercial systems at a substantially lower cost.
performance_summary.evidence: Results show that our DR agent produces reports with quality comparable to— and often exceeding— those of commercial systems (ChatGPT-5-thinking/o3/o4-mini-high Deep Research) at a substantially lower cost.

baselines.name: ChatGPT-5-thinking
baselines.evidence: Results show that our DR agent produces reports with quality comparable to— and often exceeding— those of commercial systems (ChatGPT-5-thinking/o3/o4-mini-high Deep Research).

evaluation_metrics.name: Anchored rubric scores
evaluation_metrics.application: Used to evaluate synthesis quality across model backbones and local-corpus budgets.
evaluation_metrics.evidence: An evaluation program combining anchored rubric scores, repeated A/B duels, and dry-lab validation to assess synthesis quality.

benchmark_dataset.description: No traditional benchmark dataset was used.

limitations.name: Limited Generalizability
limitations.description: The framework may not generalize well to all scientific domains due to its focus on materials discovery.
limitations.evidence: Despite progress, most agents today operate at D2–D3 depth and S1–S3 scope; they rarely sustain thousand-query retrieval runs, explicit topic-tree governance, or tight coupling to cross-domain process constraints that S3–S4 device problems demand.

future_directions.name: Expand to Other Scientific Domains
future_directions.description: Explore the applicability of the DR framework to other scientific domains beyond materials discovery.
future_directions.evidence: This work highlights a practical path toward open, secure, and scalable on-prem DR agents for automated, accelerated scientific discovery at the S3–S4 level.

resource_link.evidence: The DToR framework source code is publicly available at https://github.com/ruiding-uchicago/DToR_deep_research.
